title: pelargonium × bivulnerum
intro: Pelargonium × bivulnerum, also known as Cape Chestnut or Sore Throat Pelargonium, is a succulent shrub native to South Africa, belonging to the Geraniaceae family. The medicinal properties of Pelargonium × bivulnerum lie in its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including sore throats, coughs, and digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium × bivulnerum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve respiratory and gastrointestinal problems.
